_readme: Plan-map data. One entry per topic. Ten-second capture: append {id,title,campaign,status} â€” wire deps/links later. Viewer: /Aralia/planmap/index.html on any dev server. status: parked | specced | active | done. deps: ids this topic REQUIRES. A dep may also carry `feature: <slug>` to narrow the arrow to ONE feature of the target topic (slug = that feature's title run through the same slugifier as the ids); the `why` still explains the edge, the pointer is now structural. link: repo-relative spec doc path (opened via the dev server's /@fs bridge using repoRoot). Truth-ownership + promotion rules (who owns statuses/decisions/deps; gaps flow UP by human curation only, plan flows DOWN via planmap-to-wave): tools/agora/PLANNING-STACK.md. `open`: integer mirror of the linked subspec's '## Open' bullet count (recompute when the doc changes â€” it is NOT invented, it is counted). `spike: true`: research that could invalidate the approach (dashed âš  tile). status also allows 'superseded' (dead decision â€” drops to the Graveyard strip; add a one-line `killed` reason). `focus: true` on at most ONE topic = what Remy is actually touching now (â˜… FOCUS). Freshness fields (2026-07-14 design): `updated` (YYYY-MM-DD, tooling-written date of last real change), `tier` (strategic | component, optional), `status_note` (free-text nuance, topics and features), `decision` (boolean on features: true = waiting on human call).

topics.sub: three.js WebGPU + TSL; unlocks MIT code lifts from fable5-world-demo; VALIDATED on the RTX 2070S (37fps headless, 15.5M tris)

features.title: WebGPU probe: streamed ground scene on WebGPURenderer
Material parity pass (terrain, water, buildings)
features.note: Root cause: R3F-mounted scene lights don't drive the WebGPU LightsNode (three #30044 / r3f #2853) -> zero irradiance -> black terrain. Fixed by baking hemisphere+sun Lambert into unlit MeshBasicNodeMaterial colorNodes (no scene lights). Verified on WebGL2-fallback node path; real-GPU eyeball still pending. Sky = fog+CSS backdrop (fine); postprocessing = backlog.

features.title: Probe full-stack parity
features.note: WHY (dep on the material parity pass): parity was proven only on flat terrain + building boxes while the ground world now ships trees, grass, the full prop set, styled roofs, walls/roads/decks. The probe (?phase=webgpuprobe) now renders the SAME full stack the live scene does at the same seed/pose, reusing the game's data (chunk bundles + GroundWorld.props) with the baked-TSL Lambert path. FAIL-FAST aligned with the battle map: pre-checks navigator.gpu + requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter OR a non-WebGPU backend -> NO scene, an error pane ('WebGPU unavailable: <reason>') with one explicit 'View this scene in WebGL instead' button (routes to ?phase=world3d&ground=1 at the same pose; user-driven, never auto-fallback). Green 'WebGPU' + live FPS badge only on genuine success. Documented MISSING (WebGL-only, shown on-screen): real-time shadows on the node path (no LightsNode consumes a shadow map when lighting is baked into colorNode). Key node-path gotcha found + fixed: on InstancedMesh the InstanceNode multiplies instanceColor into the color pipeline UNCONDITIONALLY, so a dark per-instance scatter tint crushed lit trees/bushes to black â€” proven in isolation on a real WebGPU backend ([0,19,0] with dark instanceColor vs [109,158,88] without) and fixed by leaving instanceColor null (trees keep geometry leaf-green; bushes use a solid green). Headless capture correctly shows the error pane (no WebGPU adapter in headless Chromium); rendered WebGPU full-stack needs the real-GPU eyeball (RTX 2070S).

features.title: Battle-map + modal scenes migration
features.note: Battle-map WebGPU render path behind ?gpu=1 (WebGL default unchanged): BattleMap3D delegates to BattleMap3DGpuScene, same shared combat hooks/data, baked-TSL Lambert (no scene lights). FAIL-FAST preserved: probes navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() before mounting; no adapter -> NO scene, error panel + one explicit 'Use WebGL instead' button (user-driven, never auto-fallback); gl factory throws on a non-WebGPU backend; WebGPU badge only on genuine success. VISUAL PARITY PORT DONE (2026-07-05, rungs 1-4 + rung 5 wired): (1) procedural terrain texturing TRANSLATED from the onBeforeCompile GLSL to a TSL node graph (gpu/terrainColorNode.ts: per-type palettes, FBM/voronoi noise, organic edge blend, slope-rock, wet banks, dapple) â€” not a flat palette; (2) instanced grass + ground scatter (mirrors GrassLayer/GroundScatter); (3) grid + movement/path/AoE overlay translated to TSL (gpu/gridOverlayNodes.ts), fade-lerped, terrain-conforming, BattleMap3D passes validMoves/activePath/actionMode/aoeSet; (4) tokens gained team color + HP fade + selection/active rings; (5) node PostProcessing bloom+vignette wired (dynamic-imported, real-GPU-gated). Node graphs unit-tested WITHOUT a GPU (5 new tests); battle-map suites 12/8 green; tsc clean. STILL MISSING (WebGL-only, shown on-screen in a red list, no faking): real-time shadows (baked colorNode has no LightsNode for a shadow map), full animated CharacterActor rig + drei nameplates (1,491-line drei/AnimationMixer stack), GPU wind sway on grass. Captures: bm3d-port-webgl-ref.png (target look) + bm3d-port-webgpu.png (headless fail-fast pane, correct with no adapter). Rendered WebGPU battlefield still needs the real-GPU eyeball via ?gpu=1 (RTX 2070S). Modal (non-battle) scenes still parked.

topics.title: World-derived battlefield scenario lab and parity harness
topics.sub: North star chosen 2026-07-14: a battle map is a tactical projection of an actual generated location, not a separately decorated arena. Terrain, roads, water, vegetation, structures, props, occupants, factions, defenders, and encounter context descend from the same WorldForge facts; the legacy generator remains an explicit sandbox. ?step=battlemaplab rebuilds real seed/cell scenarios through the off-thread WorldForge pipeline, mounts the full combat shell, exposes World -> Region -> Local -> Ground -> Tactical provenance, compares source and tactical facts, and makes missing semantics visible. Production authority fails closed without a WorldForge projection. Every audited production launcher is now source-backed or has a distinct deterministic no-roster refusal; static authored towns, sea encounters, and location-free simulations no longer prepare substitute actors. Hostile openings author exact threat bodies, roles, approach, posture/load/facing, ecology, and one immutable combat resolution; the deterministic aftermath return removes withdrawals and preserves source bodies, site condition, and disturbance outside initiative in both 2D and WebGL 3D. Tactical relief reports selected-relative feet with five-foot contours and hillshade. Adversarial review remains unforgiving: steep cliff/slope mechanics, active/WebGPU actor parity, diagrammatic churn, dark grid-dominant terrain, pre-contact/weather/avoidance gaps, and broader pursuit/reinforcement lifecycle remain active defects.

deps.why: the scenario lab is the proof and debugging surface for the world-derived battlefield pillar
source road parity cannot pass until road geometry gains tactical terrain and movement meaning

DECISION RESOLVED: Road ambush is the first production proving ground because it forces composition, traversal, encounter framing, and source provenance to agree at once.

DECISION: On a world-derived map, must every discrete flower, rock, prop, and landmark be a source-world fact, while only continuous texture/noise/lighting may remain renderer-authored? Recommended: yes.
DECISION: Should missing source semantics fail production scenario parity while remaining visible warnings in the debug lab? Recommended: yes; production must not silently substitute plausible art.

DECISION: Should authored fords remain deterministic difficult terrain at movement cost 2, or add an Athletics check and failure risk? Recommended: keep cost 2 first; add risk only through an explicit traversal action later.
DECISION: Should a destroyed bridge persist as a WorldDelta that removes the Ground deck and invalidates tactical crossing traversal on later visits? Recommended: yes.
DECISION RESOLVED: The next canonical world-battle slice closed the TargetableMapObject gap for source features and props before moving to settlement-edge encounters.

DECISION: Should every prop catalog entry gain explicit fixed/loose mobility and weight facts before Catapult-style spells may select it? Recommended: yes; unknown facts should remain targetable by general object spells but fail restrictive movement filters.

DECISION: Should natural features displaced by roads, plots, and structures be removed or relocated in the shared Local/Ground facts instead of only being suppressed by tactical extraction? Recommended: yes so 3D Ground and combat cannot disagree about stale trees or bushes.

DECISION RESOLVED: Settlement edge and source occupants became the next canonical scenario before deeper object destruction, proving structures, population provenance, deployment, and the real combat UI together.
DECISION RESOLVED: Snapshot resident schedules at combat start and keep those tactical positions stable for the encounter; resample from the world clock after returning to exploration.

DECISION RESOLVED: Settlement-edge defenders come from generated burg, controlling-state, alert, and stationed-regiment facts; becoming enemy actors is a separate explicit-trigger-plus-matching-relation decision.

DECISION RESOLVED: A generated state regiment becomes hostile only when an explicit confrontation trigger and matching player-state relation agree; military presence, wanted status elsewhere, or bad standing without a current confrontation never silently creates enemies.

topics.title: AI-arbitrated ritual casting
topics.sub: Remy's design 2026-07-09: rituals become a REAL feature but heavily AI-arbitrated and state-aware, NOT a progress bar the player babysits. A ritual is a background narrative process; drama comes from who is around and what is happening. SAFE place -> quiet background dice roll; interruptions are social + personality-driven (a companion who doesn't get magic / isn't aware of the ritual may cause a funny distraction -> concentration roll; a quiet/shy NPC less likely, a comedian/low-INT more likely; a companion who knows the player may head off the disruptor, but only if >1 companion present). HIGHER THREAT place -> danger-driven: waylaid by enemies, or sound/visual signals surfaced via passive perception ('you hear/see X' -> player may abort); companions roll passive perception too (background per-event DC -> auto pass/fail); noticing NPCs choose investigate / sound alarm / dismiss, driven by paranoid vs careless traits. Needs: awareness model (who's near + who knows), NPC personality traits, a NEW flexible per-event DC mechanic, AI arbitration (Ollama-gated, respects no-fallback/provider-config), and the existing ritual engine's completion payoff + backlash (currently stubs). Background clock already works. Needs a brainstorm pass then phased build. FIRST PREREQUISITE BUILT 2026-07-09 (no-decision, reusable): the flexible per-event detection mechanic â€” new src/systems/perception/eventDetection.ts with EventDC + difficulty bands (5e DC ladder, tunable), passivePerceptionOf (reuses the existing calculatePassiveScore; supports PC compute + NPC passivePerception field), and resolveEventDetection (passive auto-detect >= DC, optional seeded active roll, no-fallback throw). Gate: tsc 635, 17/17 tests. This is the 'companions roll passive perception (background DC -> auto pass/fail)' piece. REMAINING pieces are ritual-specific arbitration that need the brainstorm: awareness model (who's near + who knows â€” the memory factKey from the just-shipped merge feeds 'who knows'), personality->interruption/reaction mapping, AI narration, cast-trigger + command routing, real completion payoff, backlash.

deps.why: Ritual interruption/reaction arbitration reads NPC personality (shy/comedian/paranoid/careless). Remy 2026-07-09: personality is broader than rituals, split off as its own foundational NPC-behavior track; rituals consume it.

topics.title: NPC memory: merge or keep separate
topics.sub: Two NPC-memory systems exist. LIVE: world-state NpcMemory (types/world.ts) - disposition/suspicion/plain facts/goals, filled by talking/haggling/crime/gossip, ~20 consumers, drives recruitment + dossier + prices. DORMANT: richer NPCMemory (types/memory.ts) - timestamped interactions, emotion, per-fact confidence, importance, decay; self-labeled orphan, filled only in tests. world.ts is already half-extended with the richer type's optional fields. DETERMINATION 2026-07-09 = MERGE (evidence-backed): they are the SAME system forked, not separate purposes. docs/projects/memory/GAPS.md already tracks this as G1 (schema-normalization: pick one canonical shape) + G2 (retire the orphan MemorySystem); the richer NPCMemory was the intended structured upgrade (generator TODO: 'Replace NpcMemory with the structured NPCMemory when memory models merge'), parked ~6 weeks with ZERO live writes and its one read fed a hardcoded-empty memory; world.ts was deliberately half-widened 2026-07-03 to hold the richer fields (typed loosely) awaiting exactly this call. topics.title: NPC personality system
topics.sub: Foundational NPC-behavior track, split off the ritual track by Remy 2026-07-09 ('this is more about NPC behavior, in particular their personality traits, at first'). Today characters carry only a free-text traits: string[] + real ability scores; there is NO structured personality model. Build one that drives how NPCs behave: whether a companion interrupts the player (shy/reserved less likely, boisterous/comedian more likely), how an NPC reacts to a noticed danger (paranoid investigates/alarms, careless dismisses), and more broadly dialogue tone, party dynamics, and the living-world sim. Design decisions still OPEN (brainstorm redirected here before the model was picked): numeric personality axes (Sociability/Caution/Curiosity/Impulsiveness/Confidence, behaviors derive from thresholds) vs a fixed trait-tag enum vs Big-Five. Consumed by [[ai-arbitrated-ritual-casting]] (interruption/reaction arbitration) and feeds NPC memory ([[npc-memory-reconciliation]], now merged). Needs its own brainstorm â†’ spec â†’ build.
